questions.prompt: Which amendment officially abolished slavery throughout the United States in 1865?
What was the main purpose of the Black Codes passed by Southern states after the Civil War?
choices: To grant freedmen full citizenship rights
To restrict the rights and movement of African Americans
To provide free land to former slaves
To require integrated schools in the South
questions.correctText: To restrict the rights and movement of African Americans

questions.prompt: Which president took office after Abraham Lincoln's assassination and clashed with Radical Republicans?
The 14th Amendment is best known for guaranteeing which of the following?
choices: The right to vote regardless of race
Equal protection of the laws and citizenship to people born in the U.S.
The right of women to vote
questions.correctText: Equal protection of the laws and citizenship to people born in the U.S.

questions.prompt: What system replaced slavery in the South in which farmers worked land in exchange for a share of the crop?
The Compromise of 1877 is most associated with which event?
choices: The start of the Civil War
The end of Reconstruction and removal of federal troops from the South
The passage of the Emancipation Proclamation
questions.correctText: The end of Reconstruction and removal of federal troops from the South

questions.prompt: Which amendment gave African American men the right to vote?
Andrew Carnegie made his fortune primarily in which industry?
John D. Rockefeller built a monopoly in which industry through Standard Oil?
Cornelius Vanderbilt is best known for building a vast fortune in which industry?
Which labor organization led by Samuel Gompers focused on skilled workers and 'bread and butter' issues like wages and hours?
The Haymarket Affair of 1886 in Chicago is most associated with what issue?
The 1894 Pullman Strike was broken when President Cleveland sent federal troops because the strike interfered with what?
Which port served as the main entry point for European immigrants arriving in the United States in the late 1800s and early 1900s?
What term describes the negative attitudes toward immigrants common during the late 1800s?
The Chinese Exclusion Act of 1882 was the first major U.S. law to do what?
choices: Create a path to citizenship through military service

questions.prompt: The Dawes Act of 1887 attempted to do what to Native Americans?
choices: Break up tribal land and assimilate them into white culture
questions.correctText: Break up tribal land and assimilate them into white culture

questions.prompt: The 1890 massacre at Wounded Knee in South Dakota is often considered the end of what?
choices: The major armed conflicts between the U.S. military and Native Americans
questions.correctText: The major armed conflicts between the U.S. military and Native Americans

questions.prompt: The Battle of the Little Bighorn in 1876 resulted in a defeat for which U.S. military leader?
What name was given to journalists in the Progressive Era who exposed corruption and social problems?
Upton Sinclair's book 'The Jungle' led directly to which Progressive reform?
choices: The Pure Food and Drug Act and Meat Inspection Act
questions.correctText: The Pure Food and Drug Act and Meat Inspection Act

questions.prompt: President Theodore Roosevelt's approach to large corporations earned him what nickname?
Which amendment, ratified in 1920, gave women the right to vote?
Susan B. Anthony and Elizabeth Cady Stanton are best known as leaders of which movement?
The 18th Amendment, ratified in 1919, banned what?
choices: The manufacture and sale of alcohol
questions.correctText: The manufacture and sale of alcohol

questions.prompt: The sinking of the USS Maine in 1898 helped lead the U.S. into which war?
After the Spanish-American War, the United States gained control of which group of territories?
choices: Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Philippines
questions.correctText: Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Philippines

questions.prompt: The Roosevelt Corollary added what idea to the Monroe Doctrine?
choices: European powers could colonize Latin America
The U.S. could intervene in Latin American countries to keep order
The U.S. would never get involved in Latin America
All American nations would form one government
questions.correctText: The U.S. could intervene in Latin American countries to keep order

questions.prompt: The Panama Canal, completed in 1914, was built to do what?
choices: Provide drinking water to Central America
Shorten the sea route between the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans
Connect the Mississippi River to the Gulf of Mexico
questions.correctText: Shorten the sea route between the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans

questions.prompt: Which of the following was NOT a major cause of World War I?
What event in 1917 most directly pushed the United States to enter World War I?
choices: The Zimmermann Telegram and unrestricted German submarine warfare
questions.correctText: The Zimmermann Telegram and unrestricted German submarine warfare

questions.prompt: President Woodrow Wilson's Fourteen Points called for what new international organization?
Which treaty ended World War I and harshly punished Germany?
Why did the U.S. Senate refuse to ratify the Treaty of Versailles?
choices: It freed too many German prisoners
It required the U.S. to join the League of Nations and possibly enter foreign wars
It gave too much land to France
questions.correctText: It required the U.S. to join the League of Nations and possibly enter foreign wars

questions.prompt: The 1920s African American cultural movement centered in New York City was known as what?
Langston Hughes, Zora Neale Hurston, and Duke Ellington were important figures in which movement?
The 1925 Scopes Trial in Tennessee was a famous court case about teaching what in public schools?
What event on October 29, 1929, is often considered the start of the Great Depression?
choices: The stock market crash known as Black Tuesday
The signing of the Treaty of Versailles
questions.correctText: The stock market crash known as Black Tuesday

questions.prompt: During the Great Depression, what were the shantytowns of homeless people called?
Franklin D. Roosevelt's set of programs to fight the Great Depression was called what?
Which New Deal program created jobs for young men in conservation work like planting trees and building parks?
The Social Security Act of 1935 provided benefits mainly to which groups?
choices: Retired workers, the unemployed, and people with disabilities
questions.correctText: Retired workers, the unemployed, and people with disabilities

questions.prompt: The Dust Bowl of the 1930s affected mainly which region of the United States?
What event on December 7, 1941, brought the United States into World War II?
choices: The Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor
questions.correctText: The Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor

questions.prompt: Executive Order 9066 led to the forced relocation of which group during World War II?
The D-Day invasion of June 6, 1944, took place at what location?
choices: The beaches of Normandy in France
questions.correctText: The beaches of Normandy in France

questions.prompt: President Harry Truman's decision to drop atomic bombs in August 1945 targeted which two Japanese cities?
The Holocaust was the systematic mass murder of approximately how many Jewish people by Nazi Germany?
The Tuskegee Airmen were famous for what during World War II?
choices: Being the first African American military pilots
questions.correctText: Being the first African American military pilots

questions.prompt: The Truman Doctrine of 1947 promised U.S. support to countries threatened by what?
The Marshall Plan provided billions of dollars to do what after World War II?
choices: Rebuild Western Europe to resist communism
questions.correctText: Rebuild Western Europe to resist communism

questions.prompt: NATO, formed in 1949, was created as a military alliance among which group of nations?
choices: The United States, Canada, and Western European countries
The Soviet Union and Eastern European countries
Countries in Asia and the Pacific
questions.correctText: The United States, Canada, and Western European countries

questions.prompt: The Korean War (1950-1953) ended with which result?
choices: An armistice that divided Korea near the 38th parallel
questions.correctText: An armistice that divided Korea near the 38th parallel

questions.prompt: The 1962 Cuban Missile Crisis was a 13-day confrontation between the U.S. and which country?
Senator Joseph McCarthy is best known for what during the early 1950s?
choices: Accusing many Americans of being communists, often without evidence
Negotiating the end of the Korean War
questions.correctText: Accusing many Americans of being communists, often without evidence

questions.prompt: The 1954 Supreme Court case Brown v. Board of Education ruled what unconstitutional?
Rosa Parks sparked which major civil rights protest in 1955 by refusing to give up her bus seat?
Who delivered the famous 'I Have a Dream' speech at the 1963 March on Washington?
The Civil Rights Act of 1964 outlawed discrimination based on what?
choices: Race, color, religion, sex, or national origin
questions.correctText: Race, color, religion, sex, or national origin

questions.prompt: The Voting Rights Act of 1965 banned which discriminatory practice?
choices: Literacy tests and other barriers used to keep African Americans from voting
questions.correctText: Literacy tests and other barriers used to keep African Americans from voting

questions.prompt: Malcolm X is best known for advocating which approach to civil rights?
choices: Black self-defense, self-reliance, and Black pride
Removing African Americans' right to vote
questions.correctText: Black self-defense, self-reliance, and Black pride

questions.prompt: The Black Power movement of the late 1960s emphasized what?
choices: Total assimilation into white American culture
Racial pride, political power, and economic self-sufficiency for African Americans
Strict reliance on the federal government
questions.correctText: Racial pride, political power, and economic self-sufficiency for African Americans

questions.prompt: Cesar Chavez was a leader in the fight for the rights of which group?
choices: Migrant farm workers, especially Mexican Americans
questions.correctText: Migrant farm workers, especially Mexican Americans

questions.prompt: What 1964 event led to a major escalation of U.S. involvement in Vietnam?
The 1968 Tet Offensive turned American public opinion against the Vietnam War because it showed what?
choices: That the war would soon end with victory
That North Vietnamese forces were still strong despite U.S. claims
That the South Vietnamese had surrendered
That China had entered the war
questions.correctText: That North Vietnamese forces were still strong despite U.S. claims

questions.prompt: The Vietnam War ended in 1975 with what outcome?
choices: Vietnam was split into three countries
South Vietnam fell to the North, unifying the country under communism
The U.S. won and made South Vietnam a U.S. territory
questions.correctText: South Vietnam fell to the North, unifying the country under communism

questions.prompt: The Watergate scandal led to the 1974 resignation of which president?
What was the main reason the Watergate scandal forced Nixon from office?
choices: He had affairs with foreign leaders
He covered up a break-in at the Democratic National Committee headquarters
He started an illegal war in Cuba
He refused to enforce civil rights laws
questions.correctText: He covered up a break-in at the Democratic National Committee headquarters

questions.prompt: The 26th Amendment, ratified in 1971 during the Vietnam War, lowered the voting age to what?
President Ronald Reagan's economic policy, known as 'Reaganomics,' focused mainly on what?
choices: Tax cuts, reduced regulation, and smaller domestic government
questions.correctText: Tax cuts, reduced regulation, and smaller domestic government

questions.prompt: The Berlin Wall, a major symbol of the Cold War, fell in what year?
The Soviet Union officially collapsed in what year, ending the Cold War?
The September 11, 2001 attacks were carried out by which terrorist group?
In response to 9/11, the United States launched a war in which country to target Osama bin Laden and al-Qaeda?
The U.S. invasion of Iraq in 2003 was based on claims, later proven false, that Iraq possessed what?
choices: Stockpiles of weapons of mass destruction
questions.correctText: Stockpiles of weapons of mass destruction

questions.prompt: Barack Obama, elected in 2008, made history as the first U.S. president who was what?
The 13th, 14th, and 15th Amendments are often called what?
choices: The Civil War Amendments or Reconstruction Amendments
questions.correctText: The Civil War Amendments or Reconstruction Amendments

questions.prompt: Jim Crow laws were state and local laws that did what mainly in the South?
choices: Enforced racial segregation in public places
questions.correctText: Enforced racial segregation in public places

questions.prompt: The 1896 Supreme Court case Plessy v. Ferguson established what legal doctrine?
The Great Migration in the early 1900s refers to what movement?
choices: African Americans moving from the rural South to Northern cities
Americans moving to the West Coast for gold
Native Americans being forced onto reservations
questions.correctText: African Americans moving from the rural South to Northern cities
